heading: Basis to distributees
label: 358 Basis to distributees
notes: Amendments2002—Subsec. (h)(1)(A). amended subpar. (A) generally. Prior to amendment, subpar. (A) read as follows: “which is assumed in exchange for such property, and”.Pub. L. 107–1472000—Subsec. (h). added subsec. (h).Pub. L. 106–5541999—Subsec. (3) “Certain exchanges involving ConRail” which read as follows: “To the extent provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary in the case of an exchange to which section 354(d) (or so much of section 356 as relates to section 354(d)) or section 374(c) applies, for purposes of allocating basis under paragraph (1), stock of the Consolidated Rail Corporation and the certificate of value of the United States Railway Association which relates to such stock shall, so long as they are held by the same person, be treated as one property.”Pub. L. 106–554, § 1(a)(7) [title III, § 309(c)]114 Stat. 2763Dec. 21, 2000“The Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate—“(1)shall prescribe rules which provide appropriate adjustments under subchapter K of chapter 1 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to prevent the acceleration or duplication of losses through the assumption of (or transfer of assets subject to) liabilities described in section 358(h)(3) of such Code (as added by subsection (a)) in transactions involving partnerships, and“(2)may prescribe rules which provide appropriate adjustments under subchapter S of chapter 1 of such Code in transactions described in paragraph (1) involving S corporations rather than partnerships.”

children.label: i
children.text: the fair market value of any other property (except money) received by the taxpayer,

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of any money received by the taxpayer, and

children.label: iii
children.text: the amount of loss to the taxpayer which was recognized on such exchange, and

children.label: i
children.text: the amount which was treated as a dividend, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of gain to the taxpayer which was recognized on such exchange (not including any portion of such gain which was treated as a dividend).

children.heading: Other property
children.label: 2 Other property
children.text: The basis of any other property (except money) received by the taxpayer shall be its fair market value.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, the basis determined under subsection (a)(1) shall be allocated among the properties permitted to be received without the recognition of gain or loss.

children.heading: Special rule for section 355
children.label: 2 Special rule for section 355
children.text: In the case of an exchange to which section 355 (or so much of section 356 as relates to section 355) applies, then in making the allocation under paragraph (1) of this subsection, there shall be taken into account not only the property so permitted to be received without the recognition of gain or loss, but also the stock or securities (if any) of the distributing corporation which are retained, and the allocation of basis shall be made among all such properties.

children.heading: Section 355 transactions which are not exchanges
children.label: c Section 355 transactions which are not exchanges
children.text: For purposes of this section, a distribution to which section 355 (or so much of section 356 as relates to section 355) applies shall be treated as an exchange, and for such purposes the stock and securities of the distributing corporation which are retained shall be treated as surrendered, and received back, in the exchange.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Where, as part of the consideration to the taxpayer, another party to the exchange assumed a liability of the taxpayer, such assumption shall, for purposes of this section, be treated as money received by the taxpayer on the exchange.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to the amount of any liability excluded under section 357(c)(3).

children.heading: Exception
children.label: e Exception
children.text: This section shall not apply to property acquired by a corporation by the exchange of its stock or securities (or the stock or securities of a corporation which is in control of the acquiring corporation) as consideration in whole or in part for the transfer of the property to it.

children.heading: Definition of nonrecognition property in case of section 361 exchange
children.label: f Definition of nonrecognition property in case of section 361 exchange
children.text: For purposes of this section, the property permitted to be received under section 361 without the recognition of gain or loss shall be treated as consisting only of stock or securities in another corporation a party to the reorganization.

children.heading: Adjustments in intragroup transactions involving section 355
children.label: g Adjustments in intragroup transactions involving section 355

children.text: is in a corporation which is a member of such group, and
is held by another member of such group,

children.heading: Special rules for assumption of liabilities to which subsection (d) does not apply
children.label: h Special rules for assumption of liabilities to which subsection (d) does not apply
A
children.text: which is assumed by another person as part of the exchange, and

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to which subsection (d)(1) does not apply to the assumption.

children.label: A
children.text: the trade or business with which the liability is associated is transferred to the person assuming the liability as part of the exchange, or

children.label: B
children.text: substantially all of the assets with which the liability is associated are transferred to the person assuming the liability as part of the exchange.

children.heading: Liability
children.label: 3 Liability
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “liability” shall include any fixed or contingent obligation to make payment, without regard to whether the obligation is otherwise taken into account for purposes of this title.
